extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution. Analysis: sent is set true ONLY by t2 (send_attempt), which is guarded by `not opted_out`; thus whenever sent becomes true, opted_out is false, so the implication's antecedent is false (safe). opted_out is set true ONLY by t1 (optout), which simultaneously sets sent := false, landing in a safe state. opted_out is never reset to false (no transition does so), so once true it stays true. After opted_out is true, the only enabled transitions are: t1/optout (sent stays false), t3 on send_attempt-with-opted_out (sent := false), and t4/tick (sent := false). t2 is blocked because its guard `not opted_out` is false. Therefore no transition can produce sent=true while opted_out=true, and the invariant is preserved at every step. An exhaustive BFS over all event sequences (optout, send_attempt, tick) up to length 5 from the initial state found zero violating states, confirming the property holds.
